entries.title: Initialize Rust project structure
entries.approach: Created Cargo.toml with all necessary dependencies (serde, reqwest, tokio, clap, log, anyhow, thiserror), initialized src/main.rs with basic logging setup, and created a comprehensive README.md documenting the project structure and features. Verified the project builds and compiles successfully with cargo.
learnings: Cargo with 2021 edition selected for modern Rust features
Used reqwest with async/tokio for HTTP client capabilities
Selected clap for CLI argument parsing with derive macros
Included log and env_logger for flexible logging configuration
Added thiserror for better error handling patterns

entries.title: Implement data models
entries.approach: Created comprehensive serde-compatible structs in src/models.rs for Canvas data: Course, Module, ModuleItem, File, Enrollment, Grades, VideoLink with provider detection, and utility structs for CanvasPayload, User, PayloadMetadata, ExtractionSummary, DownloadStats, and LogEntry. Included unit tests for VideoProvider detection (YouTube, Vimeo, Canvas, Other) and Course serialization. Updated src/main.rs to import the models module.
learnings: Serde derive macros make JSON serialization/deserialization seamless with #[serde(rename, default)]
VideoProvider enum with from_url() helper provides pattern matching for video source detection
Optional fields should use Option<T> with #[serde(default)] for flexible payload parsing
Test coverage for enum variants and serialization ensures data integrity
Future unused warnings are expected until these models are integrated into parser and operations
gotchas: Structs have #[serde(default)] to handle optional/missing fields in JSON payloads

entries.title: Implement payload parser
entries.approach: Created src/parser.rs with parse_payload() function that reads JSON from file path, validates file existence, handles JSON parsing errors with proper anyhow Context, and logs discovery of courses and modules. Included validate_payload() to check course structure, get_payload_stats() for extraction metrics, and comprehensive unit tests covering: valid payloads, missing files, invalid JSON, validation checks, and stats calculation. Updated src/main.rs to import parser module.
learnings: anyhow::Context provides detailed error messages with .with_context()
tempfile crate enables easy test file creation with NamedTempFile
Logging at each step (debug, info) provides operational visibility
PayloadStats struct tracks courses, modules, items, and enrollments
File existence check before reading prevents misleading error messages
gotchas: Must explicitly check file.exists() before fs::read_to_string for better error messages
serde_json::from_str() needs the full JSON payload structure to match CanvasPayload
Course ID 0 indicates invalid structure and should be rejected

entries.title: Implement logging module
entries.approach: Created src/logger.rs with comprehensive logging functions using lazy_static Mutex for thread-safe operation log storage. Implemented log_startup(), log_operation_start(), log_operation_complete(), log_operation_failed(), log_summary(), and export_logs_json(). Used chrono for timestamp generation and serde for JSON serialization. Added dependency on lazy_static and chrono to Cargo.toml. Implemented 7 unit tests with proper test isolation using setup_test() helper to filter logs by operation name and avoid cross-test contamination.
learnings: lazy_static with Mutex enables thread-safe global state in Rust
chrono::Local::now().to_rfc3339() provides ISO 8601 timestamps
Test isolation with global state requires filtering to specific test operations
OperationStatus enum with serde makes JSON output clean and queryable
log_operation() is the core function that pushes entries to the thread-safe vector
gotchas: Tests need setup_test() to clear logs and filter results, as global OPERATION_LOG persists across tests
Must derive Serialize/Deserialize on both OperationLog and OperationStatus for serde_json::to_string_pretty()

entries.title: Implement file downloader
entries.approach: Created src/downloader.rs with async HTTP downloader using reqwest and tokio. Implemented download_file() for single files with timeout support, download_module_files() for batch downloads with error handling, and create_directory_structure() for organizing files by course/module. Added cleanup_downloads() for optional maintenance. Included DownloadConfig, DownloadStats structs, and proper error handling with anyhow Context. Updated main.rs with #[tokio::main] for async support. Created 6 unit tests including async test for module downloads, directory creation tests, and stats verification.
learnings: tokio::main macro enables async entry point without manual runtime creation
#[tokio::test] macro enables async unit tests
reqwest::Client with timeout prevents hanging on unresponsive servers
File existence check before download avoids redundant work
Directory structure course_N/module_N enables clear organization
Error handling doesn't stop batch operations - failed downloads are logged but processing continues
gotchas: Must import tokio::fs::File and AsyncWriteExt for async file operations
reqwest returns bytes that need to be written asynchronously
Cleanup_downloads recursively walks directory tree so it handles nested structures

entries.title: Implement directory creation
entries.approach: Enhanced DownloadConfig struct to include both base_dir (for downloads) and output_dir (for output files), each defaulting to ./downloads and ./output respectively. Implemented initialize_directories() function that creates both directories upfront and logs completion via logger::log_operation_complete(). The function is idempotent - can be called multiple times without error. Added two new unit tests: test_initialize_directories() and test_initialize_directories_idempotent() to verify both directories are created and that the function can be safely called multiple times.
learnings: Idempotent directory operations allow safe initialization at startup
fs::create_dir_all() safely handles existing directories
Separating base_dir and output_dir allows flexible organization of downloads vs. generated files
Initialize_directories() integrates with logger for operation tracking
HashMap::new() can be used for empty details in log operations
gotchas: Must use PathBuf for flexible path composition with .join()
Directory initialization is a one-time startup operation that should be logged

entries.title: Implement grade extractor
entries.approach: Created src/extractor.rs with comprehensive grade extraction logic. Implemented extract_grades() to iterate through courses/enrollments and build ExtractedGrade structs with course_id, user_id, scores, and grades. Implemented save_grades_to_file() for JSON export with directory creation. Added get_grade_stats() using HashSet to count unique courses and users. Created extract_and_save_grades() that combines extraction and saving with proper logging integration. Included 7 unit tests covering: basic extraction, empty payloads, missing grades, file I/O, statistics calculation, and empty output handling. Updated main.rs with extractor module import.
learnings: HashSet<u64> efficiently counts unique courses and users in grade data
Conditional file write - skip file creation if no grades extracted to avoid polluting output
serde_json::to_string_pretty() produces clean, readable JSON for grade export
Parent directory creation before file write ensures output directories exist
Grade extraction integrates enrollment iteration with optional grades handling
gotchas: Must check grades.is_empty() before saving to avoid creating empty JSON files
serde requires #[serde(derive)] on both CustomStruct and surrounding types
Enrollment can exist without grades - must handle Some/None properly

entries.title: Implement video link extractor
entries.approach: Created src/videos.rs with comprehensive video extraction. Implemented extract_videos() to iterate courses/modules/items and identify video URLs (YouTube, Vimeo, mp4, Canvas media). Used VideoProvider::from_url() to detect provider type. Implemented save_videos_to_file() for JSON export. Added get_video_stats() to count videos by provider type. Created extract_and_save_videos() combining extraction with logging integration. Used regex for URL extraction, is_video_url() helper for video detection, and HashSet to ensure uniqueness. Included 8 unit tests covering extraction, provider detection, stats, file I/O, empty payloads, and URL parsing. Added regex dependency to Cargo.toml. Updated main.rs with videos module import.
learnings: HashSet<ExtractedVideo> automatically deduplicates videos by URL and provider
Regex pattern r"(https?://[^\s\)]+)" matches HTTP(S) URLs until whitespace
Provider detection uses existing VideoProvider::from_url() for consistency
VideoExtractionSummary counts by provider - YouTube, Vimeo, MP4, Other
extract_urls_from_text() provides regex-based URL extraction from text content
gotchas: Regex raw strings require escaping for special chars - use r prefix for readability
trim_end_matches() cleans trailing parens/quotes from regex results
Empty video results don't create output file - conditional save check is essential
HashSet requires PartialEq, Eq, Hash derives for items to work as set members
